title: Allantoma pluriflora

taxonomy: Allantoma pluriflora is classified within the family Sapotaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as sapotes or soapberries. It belongs to the genus Allantoma, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The species Allantoma pluriflora is further categorized as a tree or shrub, growing in the tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Allantoma pluriflora is also supported by its botanical features, such as its showy flowers and large, edible fruits. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary history and its relationships with other species within the Sapotaceae family.
